LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT YUNJI QIAN'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO: (1) REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS; (2) REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS; (3) SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES; AND (4) FOUR FORM INTERROGATORIES, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST CROSS-DEFENDANT DAVID NEMECEK JR. AND OR COUNSEL IN THE AMOUNT OF $3,500.00. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT CONFIRMED THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE COURT ADOPTS THE RECOMMENDATION OF THE PRO TEM JUDGE AS FOLLOWS: YUNJI QIAN'S MOTION IS GRANTED. QIAN & NEMECEK LLP MUST PROVIDE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION 6, 7, 8, 9, 12, 13, 14, 15 AND 16. THE BOILERPLATE, NEARLY IDENTICAL, OBJECTIONS TO EACH REQUEST ARE OVERRULED. THE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES MUST CONTAIN AN ADMISSION, DENIAL OR A STATEMENT CLAIMING AN INABILITY TO ADMIT OR DENY. QIAN & NEMECEK LLP MUST PRODUCE ALL DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO DOCUMENT REQUESTS 30, 31, AND 33. THE OBJECTIONS TO EACH REQUEST ARE OVERRULED. QIAN & NEMECEK LLP MUST PROVIDE COMPLETE AND STRAIGHTFORWARD RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES 11, 21 AND 22 AND FORM INTERROGATORIES 9.1 AND 9.2. THE OBJECTIONS TO EACH SPECIAL AND FORM INTERROGATORY ARE OVERRULED. ALL FURTHER RESPONSES AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS ARE DUE NO LATER THAN 20 DAYS AFTER SERVICE OF THE ORDER OR BY AGREEMENT BETWEEN THE PARTIES. YUNJI QIAN'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. THE COURT WILL HOLD OFF ON ISSUING AN ORDER SO THAT THE PARTIES CAN SUBMIT COMPETING PROTECTIVE ORDERS. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order For 1. Access To All Books Records Documents And Other Things Of Qian And Nemecek Llp 2. An Accounting Of Qian And Nemecek And 3. The Court To Take And Place Qian And Nemecek Llp Under The Courts Judicial Supervision For The Purpose Of Winding Up
Off Calendar Apr-06-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. DAVID W. TATE, ESQ. of Law Office of David W. Tate (Tel: 415-613-9713) appearing for Yunji Willa Qian aka Willa Leong, and Qian & Co, Inc.;
Atty. DAVID P. NEMECEK, JR., ESQ. of Qian & Nemecek LLP (Tel: 415-659-1946) appearing for Qian & Nemecek LL{ and David P. Nemecek, Jr.

The matter is argued and submitted by the parties. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

RULING: Qian's motion (1) to access the firm's books (2) compel an accounting and (3) compel judicial supervision is granted in part. Qian's request for "full access to all books, records, documents and other things of Qian & Nemecek LLP" is overbroad. Qian, however, does have a right of access to partnership documents under Corp. Code 16403(b). The court gives Qian the opportunity to make a more specific document request and the parties are ordered to meet and confer in person regarding the turnover of partnership documents. If the parties have not resolved their document dispute by April 20, 2018, then Qian may file a motion to compel with the discovery department. The motion to compel an accounting is denied without prejudice. First, "[a]n accounting cause of action is equitable in nature, and may be sought 'where ... the accounts are so complicated that an ordinary legal action demanding a fixed sum is impracticable.'" (See Civic Western Corp. v. Zila Industries, Inc. (1977) 66 Cal.App.3d 1, 14.) Qian fails to explain how she is entitled to that immediate relief without complying with CCP 437 [motion for summary adjudication]. Whether Qian is entitled to the requested accounting or whether Mandell's work is sufficient is a matter for the trial judge after observing the demeanor of the witnesses and weighing the evidence. Second, Qian's moving papers fail to demonstrate an entitlement to an accounting. The only supporting evidence is the declaration of Tate (counsel) and he does not even attach Mandell's reports. Qian improperly attempts to introduce evidence in the reply which raises due process issues. The opposing party properly objects to the Tate declaration as lacking foundation; he is not an expert/accountant. The motion for judicial supervision is denied. Qian does not show good cause for that relief under Corp. Code 16803.

The Court signs an order in open court.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR #6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify John T. Bachmayer As Counsel For Yunji Willa Qian And Qian & Co., Inc.
Off Calendar Jan-31-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
David P. Nemecek, Jr., Esq. of Qian Nemecek LLP, T:415.894.5045, for plaintiff/moving party;
David W. Tate, Esq. of the Law Office of David W. Tate, T:415.613.9713, for defendant/opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF QIAN & NEMECEK LLP's Motion To Disqualify John T. Bachmayer As Counsel For Yunji Willa Qian And Qian & Co., Inc. is denied. "The substantial relationship test is not implicated [and disqualification is not warranted ] unless the attorney was in a position where he could have received information that his former client might reasonably have assumed the attorney would withhold from his present client." (Christensen v. U.S. Dist. Court for Cent. Dist. of California (9th Cir. 1988) 844 F.2d 694, 698. Because Ms. Qian as a partner in the LLP is entitled to full access to the financial records that were reviewed by and made available to Mr. Bachmayer in the prior litigation, there is no basis to disqualify Mr. Bachmayer in this litigation. Assuming without deciding that Mr. Bachmayer had agreed to withdraw as counsel in this case, that agreement is not enforceable on a disqualification motion. The purpose of a disqualification motion is to ensure that counsel does not violate duties owed to a former client, not to enforce an asserted contract promise to withdraw.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions By Plaintiff Qian &Amp; Nemecek Llp For Sanctions Against Defendant Yunji Willa Qian, Allen Matkins Gamble Leck &Amp; Mallory, Robert Moore And Michael Betz Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 128.5
Off Calendar Mar-10-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT QIAN & NEMECEK LLP, DAVID NEMECEK JR Motion For Sanctions is denied in its entirety and the requests for sanctions by defendant Yunji Qian and her former counsel the Allen Matkins firm, Mr. Moore and Mr. Betz are also denied. Plaintiff has failed to show that Ms. Qian's resistance to Mr. Nemecek's requests to transfer trust account monies was totally and completely without merit, done for the sole purpose of harassing Mr. Nemecek or solely intended to cause unnecessary delay. Nor has Ms. Qian or her former counsel shown that the filing of this motion meets any of the requisites for imposing sanctions per CCP 128.5. This is a hotly-contested case. Serious accusations of misconduct have been leveled by the two former members of the plaintiff against each other, and there are strongly held views on both sides. The declarations of Ms. Qian and Mr. Betz in opposition to this motion suffice to show that the positions Ms. Qian took to resist the requests of Mr. Nemecek regarding the trust account funds had arguable merit. While Mr. Mandell made certain findings that favored Mr. Nemecek's positions regarding the trust account funds, those findings are neither binding on the parties nor immune from being challenged by Ms. Qian. The eventual transfer of trust accounts funds by Ms. Qian to avert an interpleader action by First Republic Bank does not show that Ms. Qian lacked arguable merit for her earlier resistance to Mr. Nemecek's requests regarding those funds. The declarations of Mr. Nemecek and Ms. Flick submitted with the moving papers suffice to show that there is arguable merit to this motion. Order signed. DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-08-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANTS QIAN & NEMECEK LLP AND DAVID NEMECEK JR'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is overruled in part and sustained in part with 20 days leave to amend. Accepting the allegations in Defendant's cross-complaint as true, he succeeds in stating a claim for misappropriation of trust funds/conversion. Defendant, however, fails to state a sufficient claim for breach of contract and breach of implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing; breach of fiduciary duty; fraud; intentional interference with contractual relations; unfair competition; accounting; and injunctive relief. A partner who has not disassociated from a partnership has the power to wind up the partnership, and the partner is entitled to continue operating the partnership until its contractual obligations are complete. (Corp. Code 16803(a).) A partner owes a partnership a fiduciary duty. A partnership does not owe a partner a fiduciary duty. Fraud has heightened pleading standards. "Something more than nonperformance is required to provide defendant's intent not to perform his partnership." (Magpali v. Farmers Group, Inc. (1996) 48 Cal.App.4th 471, 481.) Here, Nemecek representing that he and D would split profits evenly, and then not splitting profits evenly, alone does not constitute fraud. Defendant's new law firm is not a party to this lawsuit. If misappropriation of client funds occurred, it is improper for Defendant to seek to recover them. It is not unfair competition under Business and Professions Code section 17204 for a dissolved entity to conduct limited business as part of a wind down process. The Alameda Superior Court ordered Pacific Capital Transactions to conduct an Accounting. Accordingly, injunctive relief is inappropriate. The prevailing party is to provide a formal form of order and comply with CRC 3.1312.
